title: Coherent manipulation of Kondo Majoranas in two-channel Kondo setups
abstract: We study coherent manipulation of Majorana zero modes emerging in overscreened two-channel Kondo systems. Using compactified lattice models, we show that these interacting Kondo Majoranas support non-local qubits and admit teleportation, fusion, and braiding operations. In particular, we identify a distinction between non-topological and genuinely topological Y-junction geometries, the latter realizing a non-Abelian geometric holonomy. Our results establish a proof-of-principle route toward coherent control of non-Abelian anyons beyond conventional free-fermion platforms.
summary: The paper theoretically investigates how Majorana zero modes emerging from two-channel Kondo impurities can be coherently manipulated for quantum information processing. By analyzing adiabatic evolution in specific geometries (Y-junctions), the authors demonstrate that braiding operations realize non-Abelian geometric holonomy, offering a robust platform for topological quantum gates.
main_problem: The research focuses on achieving coherent manipulation of Majorana zero modes (MZMs) within two-channel Kondo setups to realize robust topological quantum operations.
main_result: The study establishes a proof-of-principle route for controlling non-Abelian anyons, demonstrating that braiding and teleportation can be achieved using the geometric holonomy associated with Y-junction geometries in these interacting systems.
method: The analysis employs theoretical techniques including mapping to compactified lattice models, adiabatic evolution protocols, and calculating Berry/Wilczek-Zee holonomies. The system dynamics are governed by complex Hamiltonians derived from Kondo interactions.
model_or_system: The physical model is the two-impurity two-channel Kondo (2CK) system coupled to chiral edge states, which is mathematically mapped onto a compactified tight-binding chain of Majorana fermions. This setup allows for the emergence of interacting Kondo MZMs.
key_observables: Ground State Degeneracy (GSD), Rabi oscillations, non-Abelian Berry holonomy, and fidelity of teleportation operations.
important_parameters: Kondo coupling asymmetry ($J_L/J_R$), chain length parity ($N$), and the degree of channel anisotropy ($\Delta H$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on mapping to a compactified description, which makes non-local degrees of freedom operationally accessible, and approximations inherent in bosonization techniques.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the two-impurity 2CK setup, energy level diagrams showing Rabi oscillations, GSD dependence on asymmetry, and schematic representations of Majorana fusion and braiding operations.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by defining the Hamiltonian for the 2CK system, analyzing its ground state degeneracy (GSD) via ED, demonstrating coherent control through adiabatic protocols (teleportation/fusion), and finally establishing that braiding is realized as a non-Abelian geometric holonomy in Y-junction geometries.
why_interesting: This work directly addresses the realization of topological quantum computation using strongly correlated electron physics, providing concrete theoretical pathways for controlling exotic anyonic states beyond simple free-fermion models.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1: (a) A two-impurity two-channel Kondo (2CK) setup using 1D edge states. (b) Compactified tight-binding chain.

FIG. 2: (a) Energy levels of the single-site model. Various transitions are highlighted. (b) Rabi oscillations of the non-local qubit between γ and the ancillary η driven by channel anisotropy. (c) The GSD of two 2CK impurities connected by a chain of N sites in the JK ≫t limit, represented by the partition function Z. (d) Kondo Majorana fusion onto the total spin of spin-singlet (⃗S1 · ⃗S2 = −3/4) or the spin-triplet (⃗S1 · ⃗S2 = 1/4) depending on qubit state, iγLγR = ∓1/2.

FIG. 3: Deterministic and coherent MZM transfer, while preserving its entanglement with an ancillary MZM η, between two spins (a1-a3), and across a spin (b1-b3) and (c1-c3). Top row panels show the adiabatic variation of the Kondo and tunnel couplings, whereas the bottom two rows show the evolution of ⟨iηγxx⟩or ⟨iηγcx⟩(blue) and ⟨iηγyy⟩ or ⟨iηγcy⟩(red) for two different initial states. Moving MZMs across spins requires a small tunneling txy, whose sign multiplies the transferred MZM entanglement (b and c).

FIG. 4: (a) Setups considered for braiding; S-centered, c-triangle, and c-centered Y-junction. (b) Braiding protocol applied to non-interacting particles on a c-centered Y-junction. (c) The parallel transport of dark fermions over a unit sphere enclosing a 4π/8 solid angle results in a robust holonomy producing topological braiding.
